title: Racosperma estrophiolatum
taxonomy: Racosperma estrophiolatum, also known as Acacia estrophiolata, is a species of shrub or small tree within the Acacia genus. This genus is classified within the subfamily Mimosoideae of the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. The family Fabaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are often characterized by their ability to form nodules on their roots to fix nitrogen. The classification of Racosperma estrophiolatum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Fabaceae family. The Acacia genus, which includes Racosperma estrophiolatum, is native to Australia and parts of Asia.

intro: Racosperma estrophiolatum, also known as the Yellow Acacia or Yellow Wattle, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to eastern Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects.
